beats.text: The edge of the known. Beyond the {setting_noun}, the {knowledge_plural} have never been recorded — {discovery_verb} by no one, mapped by no one. You carry a blank {container_noun}. The {atmosphere} ahead will fill it, if you survive long enough to make marks.

The first interior. The {guardian_noun} marked the boundary between charted and uncharted territory. You have crossed it. The map of the first level is yours — {knowledge_plural} fixed to {material} by the act of surviving them. What lies ahead is still blank.

The interior deepens. The {knowledge_plural} here do not resemble the surface maps — the {setting_noun}'s second level does not conform to expected geometry. Rooms that should not connect do. {knowledge_plural|capitalize} that should not coexist do. Your {container_noun} must account for this. Cartography requires abandoning expectation.

An anomaly. The map you have drawn does not match any archetype — the {setting_noun} has its own logic, its own {material} and {atmosphere}, its own way of connecting {knowledge_plural} that was here before any mapping system was invented. You are not recording this place. You are learning its language.

The center. Every cartographer who reached this depth said the same thing: the center moves. The final {guardian_noun} is not in a fixed location — it is wherever the dungeon's {knowledge_plural} converge at this moment. Your map is accurate. The territory has changed since you drew it.

The map complete. What was unmappable has been mapped — the {knowledge_plural} of the {setting_noun} fixed to {material} for the first time. The {atmosphere} does not acknowledge the achievement. The dungeon does not require to be known. But it has been. You have the only {container_noun} that proves it.

The map ends here — an incomplete record of the {setting_noun}'s {knowledge_plural}. Every cartographer's nightmare: a map that stops. Future cartographers will find it and understand, from the last mark, exactly where you ran out of {knowledge_noun}. They will try to continue from that point. Some will.

branches.text: The second level does not appear on any existing map. You are the first instrument through here. Record carefully.
An injured cartographer makes imprecise marks. The map will be less accurate. The {knowledge_plural} will be less reliable. Push on anyway — an imprecise map is better than none.
The territory becomes familiar. The map anticipates itself — the {knowledge_plural} ahead already half-drawn from the patterns of the ones behind. This is what mastery does to cartography.
